categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings releases, revenue and EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividend announcements, and market cap milestones.

categories.label: AI & Product Strategy
categories.description: Gemini model launches, Trillium chip, AlphaFold breakthroughs, Google I/O announcements, Pixel hardware, and AI partnerships with firms like Mistral and Anthropic.

categories.label: Antitrust & Regulatory Actions
categories.description: DOJ search monopoly case, EU DMA investigations, UK CMA probes, Epic Games verdict, ad tech trials, potential forced breakup, and global antitrust fines.

categories.label: Cloud & Infrastructure Investment
categories.description: Data center construction worldwide, cloud partnership deals, nuclear and clean energy procurement, and international expansion in Malaysia, Thailand, India, and the UK.

categories.label: Corporate Strategy & Leadership
categories.description: M&A activity including Wiz and HubSpot talks, Character.AI licensing, new CFO appointment, layoffs, organizational restructuring, and Waymo autonomous vehicle expansion.

categories.label: Advertising & Revenue Ecosystem
categories.description: Ad tech practices and disputes, third-party cookie deprecation, ad sales restructuring, YouTube monetization, publisher licensing conflicts, and Google Play store policies.

categories.label: Data Privacy & Content Safety
categories.description: Privacy lawsuits and settlements, children's online safety litigation, Gemini AI bias controversies, content moderation actions, data protection investigations, and misinformation campaigns.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, Nasdaq and S&P 500 movements, tech sector rallies or selloffs, macro indicators, and articles mentioning GOOGL as part of a wider market story.

categories.label: Analyst Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades and downgrades, price target revisions, buy/sell/hold ratings, institutional positioning changes, and broker commentary on valuation.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial operational detail or passing mention with no revenue or margin implications, 10=directly and substantially impacts revenue, margins, or total addressable market
1=outcome or content fully expected and already priced into consensus expectations, 10=outcome or revealed information completely unexpected by market participants
1=impact limited to the next few trading days, 10=structural shift affecting the company's trajectory for years
1=neutral or flat tone with no clear positive or negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no new data, 10=rich in hard data including specific financial figures, dates, and quantified outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for the company with no reasonable counter-reading
1=affects only this company, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=macro or market-wide event
1=no effect on competitive positioning, 10=fundamentally reshapes the competitive landscape in a key business segment
1=no regulatory or legal dimension, 10=major legal or regulatory action that could force structural business changes
1=transient story that resolves or is forgotten within a day, 10=persistent story that remains in investor discourse for weeks or months
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis, 10=fundamentally challenges or changes the investment narrative
1=rehash or incremental follow-up of a widely known ongoing story, 10=first report of genuinely new information
1=consensus view with no meaningful disagreement among investors, 10=deeply divisive among investors with strong arguments on both sides
1=no financial results reported or results exactly in line with prior guidance and consensus, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss analyst expectations as described in the article

kept.materiality: Ranges from passing mentions in market roundups to DOJ breakup proposals and $23 billion acquisition talks that directly threaten or expand core business segments.
kept.surprise: Ranges from pre-announced conference dates and expected quarterly reports to the unexpected DOJ breakup filing, Gemini bias controversy, and the sudden Character.AI talent acquisition.
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from single-day market roundups and Russian fines to the DOJ monopoly ruling and nuclear energy partnerships that reshape the business for a decade.
kept.sentiment_strength: Ranges from neutral conference announcements and investor day webcasts to strongly negative DOJ breakup coverage and strongly positive first-ever dividend and $2 trillion valuation articles.
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line trending summaries and opinion pieces to earnings releases packed with revenue, EPS, margin, and segment breakdowns.
kept.directional_clarity: Ranges from ambiguous events like the Anthropic investment scrutiny, where competitive benefit and regulatory risk coexist, to unambiguously positive earnings beats and unambiguously negative antitrust losses.
kept.scope: Ranges from company-only events like CFO appointment and Waymo incidents to sector-wide DMA investigations affecting all Big Tech and macro-level global tax negotiations.
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from routine partnership announcements to Gemini launch directly competing with OpenAI, DOJ search remedies threatening Google's core moat, and the Wiz acquisition bid strengthening cloud security positioning.
kept.regulatory_risk: Massive variation from zero-regulatory product launches to the DOJ seeking full breakup, EU upholding $2.7 billion fine, UK CMA ad tech findings, and DMA non-compliance investigations across multiple jurisdictions.
kept.expected_duration: Ranges from one-day market roundup mentions to the DOJ antitrust saga, EU DMA investigations, and recurring Waymo safety concerns that persist across many months of the corpus.
kept.narrative_shift: Ranges from data center investments reinforcing the established AI-capex narrative to the DOJ breakup proposal and the August monopoly ruling which fundamentally altered the risk profile discussion.
kept.repeatedness: Clear variation between first-break stories like the Wiz acquisition report and Gemini launch versus fourth or fifth updates on the same EU fine or antitrust ruling within hours.
kept.controversy: Ranges from consensus-positive events like earnings beats to deeply divisive topics like whether a DOJ breakup would unlock or destroy value, and whether massive AI capex represents visionary investment or wasteful spending.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Directly relevant given four quarterly earnings cycles in the corpus with varied outcomes: Q4 2023 disappointed on cloud revenue despite strong EPS, Q1 2024 beat decisively triggering the first dividend, and Q3 2024 beat on cloud growth.

dropped.management_signal: While a few articles cover the CFO hire and Gemini team reorganization, the vast majority of articles have no leadership dimension, making this uniformly low across the corpus and better captured through materiality and narrative_shift.
dropped.actionability: As a mega-cap with deep liquidity, nearly all significant GOOGL news is immediately actionable by institutional investors, producing insufficient variation to be informative as a separate dimension.
